title: pilosella densiflora subsp. umbellifera
intro: Pilosella densiflora subsp. umbellifera, also known as a subspecies of the common knapweed, is a flowering plant native to certain regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and reducing inflammation. The health benefits of Pilosella densiflora subsp. umbellifera include its potential to aid in digestion, relieve menstrual cramps, and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
